revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ajiyoshi Futagoyama Kofun]][[ja:味美二子山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kasugai, Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name =Ajiyoshi Futagoyama Kofun | native_name =二子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Ajiyoshi Futagoyama kofun01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ajiyoshi Futagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ajiyoshi Futagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.226111|N|136.936667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kasugai|qid=Q26101|1=Kasugai, Aichi}} | region = {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|qid=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (on site museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Ajiyoshi Futagoyama Kofun'''|味美二子山古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Futago neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kasugai, Aichi|en|Kasugai, Aichi|qid=Q26101|lt=Kasugai, Aichi|1=Kasugai, Aichi}} in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|qid=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201856|title=二子山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=}}</ref> It is the largest of a cluster of ''kofun'', including the Ajiyoshi Hakusan Jinja Kofun and the Gotosho Kofun, which together with the Ajiyoshi Kasugayama Kofun located about 500 meters to the northwest, forms the Ajiyoshi Kofun Cluster. ==Overview== The Ajiyoshi Futagoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. With a length of 96 meters and a height of six meters, it is estimated to have been constructed in the middleof the 6th century AD. The tumulus was constructed in two tiers, and is orientated 30 degrees to the northwest. The tumulus had a structure on the northeast side, possibly a ceremonial platform, and was originally surrounded by a shield-shaped moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The tumulus was burglarized in a large scale in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. Per an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1935, a hollow with a diameter of four to five meters was found in the posterior circular portion, which is where the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was probably located, but no {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} or [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s could be found. In 1967, a {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|qid=Q830305|lt=Japan Air Self-Defense Force|1=Japan Air Self-Defense Force}} training aircraft crashed next to the ''kofun'', causing considerable damage. During the restoration work, numerous ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were found, some in the forms of horses, people, waterfowl, etc., in addition to the more common cylindrical varieties. Penetration into the interior of the ''kofun'' was hampered by seepage of ground water, and subsequent work was suspended. In 1991, further excavations were conducted in conjunction with the construction of the surrounding Futagoyama Park, resulting in the discovery of more artifacts, including more ''haniwa'' and examples of {{ill|Mino ware|en|Mino ware|qid=Q11609247|lt=Mino ware|1=Mino ware}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. {{ill|X-ray fluorescence|en|X-ray fluorescence|qid=Q898974|lt=Fluorescent X-ray analysis|1=X-ray fluorescence}} revealed that this pottery had been fired at the Shinohara ancient kiln site eight kilometers to the north.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A small museum, the {{nihongo|'''Haniwa no Yakata'''|ハニワの館|qq=}}, has been built at the site to house these items. It is located about 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|qid=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}} [[:en:Tōkai Transport Service Jōhoku Line|Johoku Line]] {{ill|Ajiyoshi Station (Meitetsu)|en|Ajiyoshi Station (Meitetsu)|qid=Q4699833|lt=Ajiyoshi Station|1=Ajiyoshi Station (Meitetsu)}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 54 meters long x 45 meters wide ;Constriction width: 38 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 48 meter diameter x 8 meters high ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|qid=Q9652090|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== [[Category:broken commons categories]] *[https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kunisitei/0857.html Aichi Prefecture Cultural Properties ] *[https://www.city.kasugai.lg.jp/shimin/bunka/bunkazai/midokoro/1004372.html Kasugai City official site]
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
